page.content:
Table 1 presents data on various biochemical markers in two groups: Air and Wood smoke. The Reduced GSH (μM/mg protein) is 25.9 (19.3 - 29.3) for Air and 22.8 (20.2 - 27.5) for Wood smoke. The percentage of GSSG is 7.4 (4.5-9.9) in Air compared to 9.0 (6.6-15.8) in Wood smoke. Ascorbate levels (μM/mg protein) are 8.07 (6.35 - 10.1) for Air and 8.02 (6.67 - 10.2) for Wood smoke. The percentage of DHA is 23.1 (17.5-32.9) in Air and 23.0 (15.4-33.0) in Wood smoke. Urate levels (μM/mg protein) are 1.59 (1.09 - 2.24) in Air versus 1.80 (0.11 - 2.85) in Wood smoke. HO-1 (ng/mg protein) measures 7.32 (4.48 - 9.95) in Air and 7.52 (5.34 - 9.89) in Wood smoke. Total Protein (mg/ml) is 0.12 (0.09 - 0.16) for both groups, and Glutathione S-transferase (U/mg protein) is 0.55 (0.39 - 0.82) for Air and 0.59 (0.37 - 0.84) for Wood smoke. 

Table 2 details demographic and clinical characteristics across five groups: Controls, Inactive UC, Active UC, Inactive CD, and Active CD. The sample sizes are 10, 5, 6, 5, and 2 respectively.

page.content: The id numbers for Controls are c11, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c12, c14, c16, c17; for Inactive UC, they are p10, p23, p26, p27, p32; for Active UC, they are p7, p8, p13, p19, p22, p25; for Inactive CD, p11, p15, p18, p20, p31; and for Active CD, p29, p30. Gender distribution shows 6/4, 2/3, 5/1, 1/4, and 2/0 respectively. Mean ages are 27 (21–33), 40 (37–54), 42 (34–71), 48 (34–65), and 48. The localization of disease includes none for Controls, Proctosigmoid colon and pancolitis for Inactive UC, rectum and proctosigmoid colon for Active UC, descending colon and proctosigmoid colon for Inactive CD, and colon with skip lesions and proctosigmoid colon for Active CD. Medications vary, with Controls having none, Inactive UC including 5-ASA and azathioprine, Active UC having 5-ASA, Inactive CD having none and 5-ASA, and Active CD having 5-ASA and none.
